content.text: I'll analyze the baseline: weights array has the BRAM attributes but BRAM18_equiv=0. The issue is the async reset on `weight_q` (`if (!rst_n) weight_q<=8'sd0;`) — Xilinx BRAM output registers don't support async reset, so Vivado falls back to distributed LUTRAM for the entire weight array. Fix: move the weight read into its own always block without async reset, leaving the FSM gating unchanged (`mac_valid_q1/q2` still controls when the MAC product is accumulated).

retrospector_advice.analysis: Attempt 1 left the single-cycle ROM read (just moved it to a reset-free always block) — Vivado still inferred distributed LUTRAM because the array is too small/shallow per port and no address register was present, so BRAM count stayed at 0. Attempt 2 added the canonical registered-address + registered-output BRAM pattern (weight_addr_r -> weights[] -> weight_q) and extended the MAC pipeline by one stage, but Verilator caught a latency/scheduling mismatch: the address path now takes 2 cycles before weight_q is valid, yet act_q1 is paired with weight_q in the same cycle as the multiply — there is a one-cycle phase error between the activation pipeline and the weight pipeline, plus the drain condition still allows ST_RUN to leave before the deepened pipeline has fully flushed (mac_valid_q0/q1/q2 don't cover the new q0->addr_r->q_out latency, dropping the final issued MAC).
retrospector_advice.suggestion: Keep the registered-address + registered-output BRAM template (it is what forces block-RAM inference), but realign the activation pipeline to match the new 2-cycle ROM read latency: introduce act_q2 so the multiply consumes (weight_q, act_q2) — i.e. activation must be delayed exactly as many cycles as the address-then-data ROM path (2), not 1. Concretely shift lane/valid through q0->q1->q2 alongside act, multiply at q2 using weight_q (which corresponds to addr issued at q0), then accumulate at q3; extend the drain guard to require !mac_valid_q0 && !mac_valid_q1 && !mac_valid_q2 && !mac_valid_q3 so no issued MAC is dropped. Do NOT collapse back to a single-cycle read (loses BRAM); do NOT touch SCALE_MULT/SHIFT, bias, rounding, or the ST_BIAS/ST_SCAL/ST_OUT/ST_EMIT sequencing (correctness-critical). Also drop the `ram_style="block"` attribute on the weights array (only `rom_style="block"` is needed for a ROM) to avoid Vivado downgrading to LUTRAM when both attributes conflict, and keep biases as block ROM too so the BRAM18 count is unambiguously > 0.
